timeline.message:
Run started for task: Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: coding-S1-ahc001-atcoder-ad
Title: S1. AHC001 / AtCoder Ad

Problem:
Place many non-overlapping axis-aligned ad rectangles in a 10000 x 10000 square so each advertiser gets a rectangle containing its requested point and with area close to its requested target area.

Grading target:
Continuous score from advertiser satisfaction.
Higher is better.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>

Node started: Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: coding-S1-ahc001-atcoder-ad
Title: S1. AHC001 / AtCoder Ad

Problem:
Place many non-overlapping axis-aligned ad rectangles in a 10000 x 10000 square so each advertiser gets a rectangle containing its requested point and with area close to its requested target area.

Grading target:
Continuous score from advertiser satisfaction.
Higher is better.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>

Node started: Given a list of N requested points (x_i, y_i) and target areas (A_i) within a 10000x10000 grid, perform a recursive spatial decomposition (such as a k-d tree or a quadtree approach). The task is to partition the 10000x10000 space into N non-overlapping rectangular cells, where each cell contains exactly one requested point. The output must be a list of N rectangular boundaries, each defined by its coordinates: [(x_min_1, y_min_1, x_max_1, y_max_1), ..., (x_min_N, y_min_N, x_max_N, y_max_N)].

Node started: Given a list of N bounding rectangles (x_min_i, y_min_i, x_max_i, y_max_i) from the spatial partitioner, a list of N requested points (x_i, y_i), and a list of N target areas (A_i), refine each rectangle to maximize advertiser satisfaction. For each index i, find a new rectangle (rx_min_i, ry_min_i, rx_max_i, ry_max_i) such that: 1) The rectangle is contained within the bounding rectangle from the first helper. 2) The point (x_i, y_i) is inside the new rectangle. 3) The area of the new rectangle is as close to A_i as possible. The output must be a list of N refined rectangles: [(rx_min_1, ry_min_1, rx_max_1, ry_max_1), ..., (rx_min_N, ry_min_N, rx_max_N, ry_max_N)].
